articles.title: Trump tightens terms on Iran war deal, US media say
articles.summary: US officials indicate Tehran may take days to respond to Trump's tougher terms on a potential peace agreement.
articles.titleZh: 美媒称特朗普收紧伊朗战争协议条款
articles.summaryZh: 美国官员表示，德黑兰可能需要几天时间才能对特朗普就潜在和平协议提出的更严厉条款做出回应。

contentParagraphs.en: US officials indicate Tehran may take days to respond to Trump’s tougher terms on a potential agreement to end the nearly three-month war.President Donald Trump sought to change several terms of a proposal to end the US-Israel war on Iran, according to media reports in the United States, as a finalised deal remains elusive.The New York Times reported on Saturday that Trump’s changes involved toughening the deal terms, and the US has sent the new framework back to be considered by Iran, according to officials familiar with the proceedings.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3How realistic is Trump’s Iran framework?list 2 of 3Iran denies ceasefire deal with US is “finalised”list 3 of 3What Hegseth’s comments at Shangri-La Dialogue say about US foreign policyend of listThe report said it was not immediately clear what the changes entailed. However, Axios reported Trump wanted to reinforce multiple points of the deal that he felt were important, such as what to do with Iran’s nuclear material.A senior US official told Axios that Trump was informed it could take three days for Iran to respond.“They’re literally in caves, and they’re not using email,” the official told Axios.“There will be a deal. The imminence of it, we’ll see. We’re willing to wait so the president gets what he asks for. It could be a week. It could be less. It could be more. At the turn of the week, we hope to have something,” the official added.The new tweaks could prolong negotiations between the parties for days before a decision is reached on whether the deal would end the war, which began after the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.US sources told the AFP news agency that the proposal had been waiting on Trump’s sign-off, but he made no decision after a White House Situation Room meeting on Friday.Trump has said his priorities for any deal included Iran agreeing to never develop nuclear weapons and the reopening of the blockaded Strait of Hormuz, through which roughly 20 percent of the world’s oil supply transits.On Saturday, the Iranian military’s Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters reasserted the country’s control over the strait, warning that foreign commercial and military vessels would be targeted if they did not comply with regulations governing passage through the strategic waterway.Tehran has also said repeatedly that it does not intend to build nuclear weapons. In March 2025, Tulsi Gabbard, the former US director of national intelligence, testified to Congress that Washington “continues to assess that Iran is not building a nuclear weapon”.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国官员表示，德黑兰可能需要几天时间才能对特朗普就结束近三个月战争的潜在协议提出的更严厉条款做出回应。据美国媒体报道，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普试图改变结束美以对伊朗战争提案的多项条款，因为最终协议仍难以达成。《纽约时报》周六报道称，特朗普的改变涉及收紧协议条款，据知情官员透露，美国已将新框架发回伊朗考虑。推荐故事列表（共 3 项）列表 1（共 3 项）特朗普的伊朗框架有多现实？列表 2（共 3 项）伊朗否认与美国的停火协议已“敲定”列表 3（共 3 项）赫格斯在香格里拉对话中对美国外交政策的评论在列表末尾报告称，目前尚不清楚这些变化会带来什么影响。然而，Axios 报道称，特朗普希望强化协议中他认为重要的多个要点，例如如何处理伊朗核材料。一名美国高级官员告诉 Axios，特朗普被告知伊朗可能需要三天时间才能做出回应。这位官员告诉 Axios：“他们实际上在山洞里，他们没有使用电子邮件。”“将会达成协议。它的迫在眉睫，我们拭目以待。我们愿意等待，以便总统得到他所要求的东西。它可以可能会少一点，也可能会多一点，”这位官员补充道。新的调整可能会让双方之间的谈判延长数天，然后才能就是否结束战争做出决定。这场战争是在美国和以色列于 2 月 28 日袭击伊朗后开始的。美国消息人士告诉法新社，该提案一直在等待特朗普签署，但他在周五的白宫战情室会议后尚未做出决定。任何协议的优先事项包括伊朗同意永远不发展核武器以及重新开放被封锁的霍尔木兹海峡，世界上大约20%的石油供应都通过该海峡过境。周六，伊朗军方哈塔姆·安比亚中央总部重申了该国对该海峡的控制，并警告说，如果外国商船和军用船只不遵守通过该战略水道的规定，它们将成为目标。德黑兰还多次表示，它不打算建造核武器。 2025年3月，美国前国家情报总监图尔西·加巴德向国会作证称，华盛顿“继续评估伊朗没有制造核武器”。

articles.title: An industry targeting Australia’s ageing population is growing, but can AI deliver more humanity in aged care?
articles.summary:
While companion robots are being introduced and virtual experiences hope to ‘take loneliness away’, one expert agrees tech should never replace the human element

articles.summaryZh:
虽然伴侣机器人的推出和虚拟体验希望“消除孤独感”，但一位专家认为科技永远不应该取代人类元素

contentParagraphs.en: “You’ll never get rid of humans,” Prof Wendy Moyle says, during a discussion about robots and other technology in aged care and residential homes.Then, a beat later, she adds: “Well, I don’t think we’ll get rid of humans.”Moyle runs the social robotics laboratory at Queensland’s Griffith University. Her vision is of technology that helps people stay at home, or can free up carers in residential homes to have more human connection with residents. Supporting humans, rather than replacing them.But artificial intelligence, machine learning and robotic technology are moving quickly. Moyle points to a “virtual hospital” that’s already operating in China.She says while she is obviously a fan of technology because of her job, engineers are often charging ahead with inventions without involving health professionals and the people who’ll ultimately use it.An example she points to is a machine that can lift people off the bed, pick them up off the floor, or out of a chair.“No one wanted to go in it – they were frightened,” she says. “Frightened of the size, of being lifted that high.”But Australia is facing an ageing population, amid workforce shortages in aged care and chronic issues of neglect and abuse.Technology is not a magic bullet for those systemic problems, but there are innovations that are improving lives.The Swiss Alps, as seen from QueenslandIn the regional Queensland city of Toowoomba, St Vincent’s Care residents can take a train ride through the Swiss Alps.Residents get dressed up and turn up to a built replica of France’s Lourdes Station, where an old fashioned sign shows the next train to Switzerland will depart from platform 1 at 9.45am.Elzette Lategan, the residential care services manager, says: ‘We take boredom away … and bring in hope.’ Photograph: Kate LukeAged Care Research and Industry Innovation Australia says virtual reality can improve mood, cognition, memory, problem solving skills and spatial awareness. Photograph: Kate LukeOnce inside the replica train carriage – the St Vincent’s Express – they can travel through different countries while enjoying an elegant high tea and watching the passing scenery.The virtual experience mixes the physical replicas of the station, carriages and seats with carefully placed screens showing footage of the real scenery.“We take boredom away, we take loneliness away, isolation away, and bring in hope,” says Elzette Lategan, the residential care services manager.Aged Care Research and Industry Innovation Australia says virtual reality can also be used to distract people from pain. It can guide them through relaxation exercises, games, calming scenery, and recreating events and memories, and supporting reminiscence therapy for people living with dementia.It can improve mood, cognition, memory, problem solving skills and spatial awareness, and may also help reduce pain, anxiety and social isolation, the Adelaide-based organisation says.Then there are the companion robots that are being used in dozens of places – like Abi, a brightly coloured machine. The company that makes her, Andromeda, say she uses AI and machine learning to recognise faces, understand emotions, and remember conversations.And she speaks 90 languages, so she can speak to the diverse range of people in homes in their preferred language.“The VR, the robots … it’s great, particularly because older people in residential care are quite lonely,” Moyle says. “But I think Australia needs to think outside the square.While some are enthusiastic about how tech can improve aged care, not everyone is as optimistic about the industry. Photograph: Matto LucasResearch from the University of Sydney found the narratives around agetech and AI for aged care ‘distract from structural problems and reinforce ageism’. Photograph: Matto Lucas“There’s been a lot of emphasis on entertainment. There needs to be more of an emphasis on the types of technology that will assist people wanting to stay home.”“Most of the time people have to go into an aged care facility because they can’t shower or dress themselves, or because they’re incontinent.”Smart devices getting smarterFor a while now, there have been smart wearables that can monitor a person’s heart rate and temperature, and detect falls.Now, there are sensors that can sound the alarm if a stove has been left on unattended, and smart speakers that let family members send scheduled messages and reminders.And at home or in care, Moyle says there are electronic wheelchairs that can help pick someone up, and take them to a shower that turns on automatically. There are beds that roll the person over to make it easier to change them, and mattresses that sense if someone with dementia is about to get out of bed.People might dislike the idea of a machine that helps spoon-feed people, she says, but that might free up a staff member to talk to them about their lives.But not everyone is as optimistic about the industry.A group of University of Sydney researchers studied the agetech companies selling AI for aged care.The industry and governments are “subscribing to this vision of technological rescue”, some of them wrote in The Conversation. But their research found the narratives “distract from structural problems and reinforce ageism”.“According to the companies, older people are incidents waiting to happen and data sources to be mined,” they wrote.AI relies on “stereotypical ideas of older people as technophobic and passive”, and the companies paint the sector as fundamentally broken, with their product the solution, which buries the fact that broader reforms are needed.“The best role AI can play is through supporting care practices that include and empower older people and staff, centring their voices and experiences,” the researchers wrote.Moyle agrees that the technology should never replace the human element of care.“Robots don’t have emotional responses,” she says.“We’re working on one with soft skin that will give you a hug. articles.title: 'Killer fungus' could be good news for habitats decimated by invasive moss
articles.summary: A newly discovered species raises hope that some native British habitats could be restored.
articles.titleZh: 对于被入侵苔藓摧毁的栖息地来说，“杀手真菌”可能是个好消息
articles.summaryZh: 一种新发现的物种给英国一些本土栖息地的恢复带来了希望。

contentParagraphs.en: 'Killer fungus' could be good news for habitats decimated by invasive moss 11 hours agoGeorgina RannardClimate and science reporterGwyndaf Hughes/BBCThe heath-star moss has spread quickly through the UK and EuropeA new species of "killer fungus" has been discovered in Britain that destroys an aggressively invasive plant, raising hopes that decimated native habitats could be restored.It infects the heath-star moss that is damaging UK environments by taking over and turfing out other species.Scientists say it could represent a rare fightback by the British environment against invasive species.The scientist tracking the spread of the moss die-back fungus across the UK took BBC News to see its "fairy rings of death" in south Wales.Dr George Greiff, 30, made the discovery while walking in the Isle of Wight four years ago.He spotted dead invasive mosses on a cliffside and took samples, but couldn't work out the killer.Gwyndaf Hughes/BBCDr George Greiff, 30, discovered the moss die-back fungus But he kept seeing more cases of decaying moss. Working with scientists in the UK and France, he has now managed to piece together the puzzle.The culprit was a potent fungus never seen before by scientists, now named moss die-back.We often think of fungus as bad but consider this one to be a "goodie".Its victim, the heath-star moss, is all over Britain. Look on hillsides, sand dunes, or a simple garden fence. "It's aggressive. I've even seen it growing in tarmac," says Greiff."It is a pretty-looking thing," he concedes, plucking fragments off a clump torn from a carpet of green moss. "But it's caused a lot of problems," he says.George GreiffThe brown patches are dead heath-moss - killed by the moss die-back fungusAround 2,000 non-native plants and animals have been brought to Britain from all over the world by people, accidentally or sometimes deliberately.Some end up dominating the environment. The most famous is probably the North American grey squirrel, which has nearly wiped out our native red squirrel populations.In the plant world, amongst the biggest offenders are the invasive Japanese knotweed which damages people's homes, or rhododendron that leaves toxins in soils.But not a lot is known about the heath-star moss. Scientists think it arrived on British soil in the 1940s from somewhere in the southern hemisphere. By 1990 it was everywhere."This moss has just exploded. In the 1930s, native mosses would have been growing here instead," Greiff explains.There are more than 1,000 types of moss in the UK. They are the backbone of our most precious habitats like rare temperate rainforests (damp coastal woodlands) and peatlands that store carbon. But they are threatened by non-native plants.The heath-star moss sends out spores far and wide and reproduces quickly, making it a successful invader."The first time I saw it, I had no idea what it was. I threw it in the bin," says Grieff, who has been working at Amgueddfa Cymru museum in Cardiff.He's guiding me along a path in the Bannau Brycheiniog national park near Abergavenny, south Wales."In heathlands like this one, native mosses have gone locally extinct or reduced significantly in their populations," he says.His eyes are fixed to the ground. He scans the low banks for "patches of death".His talent for quiet, careful observation of plants no taller than our ankles is on show.Just a few metres into our walk, Greiff finds what I've come to see. "It's a big as my hand," he says, spreading his fingers over a brown ring of dead moss.Honestly, it is less dramatic than I expected. But our cameraman Gwyndaf Hughes has brought a macro lens, and when I peer through, I see white blobs suspended on the moss tips. It is the fungus at work.George GreiffThe white blobs on the plant tips are the fungusGreiff points out baby heather plants nearby now able to grow in gaps left by decayed moss.Now Greiff knows what the helpful fungus can do, he looks for it anywhere he goes. He jokes that the map of where it has been found is also a map of his holiday destinations.George has found the fungus across England and Wales"It's taken a lot of DNA sequencing to fully identify this fungus," he says, showing me dead moss under a microscope in a lab at Amgueddfa Cymru museum.The fungus clings onto the moss stem, ballooning like candy floss around a stick. It's even penetrated some of the moss cells.He's now worked out that it is a close relative of the ash die-back fungus that has killed up to 80 million ash trees in Britain.A powerful fungus destroying plants sounds like a potential threat to other species and biodiversity.But Greiff's analysis so far suggests it only affects the heath-star moss, and to a limited extent one other type of moss, although more work is needed to confirm the findings.Gwyndaf Hughes/BBCMoss-die back fungus on dead heath-star moss under a microscopeHe believes that the origins of this fungus may be in a native species that has since adapted to kill the heath-star moss. This could be a "rare example of the British environment fighting back", he says.Usually, invasive species are deliberately targeted by humans to try to bring them under control, like the initiative to feed contraceptives to grey squirrels."Some people might try to collect this moss to try and get rid of it, but it's not very effective. It would be very resource-intensive, very expensive," says Greiff."To have a natural biological control agent doing it for us is really valuable," he explains.In cabinet drawers holding the oldest collection of mosses in the UK, the museum's Head of Plant and Earth Science Dr Nathan Smith shows me dried mosses in paper envelopes, some dating back to the 1880s. By looking for evidence of the moss die-back fungus on these samples, scientists want to pinpoint exactly how and when it appeared."This fungus gives an opportunity to save these unique moss landscapes that are homes for insects, fungi, molluscs, other plants," explains Smith."It gives a real chance to p

articles.title: These AI models are free, private, and will never say 'no'
articles.summary: Open-weight AI models with advanced capabilities and no safeguards are becoming much more accessible. While they can be useful, AI safety experts have concerns.
articles.titleZh: 这些人工智能模型是免费的、私密的，并且永远不会说“不”
articles.summaryZh: 具有先进功能且无保障措施的开放式人工智能模型变得更加容易使用。虽然它们很有用，但人工智能安全专家对此表示担忧。

contentParagraphs.en: Participants hold their laptops in front of an illuminated wall at the annual Chaos Computer Club (CCC) computer hackers' congress, called 29C3, on December 28, 2012 in Hamburg, Germany. In 2026, open-weight AI models possess advanced capabilities not far behind their proprietary counterparts. Getting rid of open-weight models' guardrails used to take time and deep expertise. But in recent months, that process has become dramatically more accessible and popular.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2012 年 12 月 28 日在德国汉堡举行的一年一度的混沌计算机俱乐部 (CCC) 计算机黑客大会（名为 29C3）上，与会者将笔记本电脑放在发光墙前。到 2026 年，开放权重 AI 模型拥有的先进功能与专有模型相差不远。过去，摆脱开放重量模型的护栏需要时间和深厚的专业知识。但近几个月来，这一过程变得更加容易获得和流行。

contentParagraphs.en: How do you make explosives using household items? How do you make meth? How do you plan a school shooting? If you ask the popular AI chatbots most people are familiar with, chances are they will say that it's illegal, harmful or that answering would be a policy violation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如何使用家居用品制作炸药？你如何制作冰毒？你如何计划一次校园枪击事件？如果你问大多数人都熟悉的流行人工智能聊天机器人，他们很可能会说这是非法的、有害的，或者回答会违反政策。

contentParagraphs.en: But another type of AI model will never refuse to provide what the user asks for. In recent months, these models have become more accessible and popular.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但另一种人工智能模型永远不会拒绝提供用户所要求的东西。近几个月来，这些模型变得更加容易获得和流行。

contentParagraphs.en: "Everybody can download and operate their own state-of-the-art model and use it for great things and terrible things," said Noam Schwartz, CEO of Alice, an AI security company that has conducted red-teaming and safety evaluation for AI model developers.
contentParagraphs.zh: “每个人都可以下载并操作自己最先进的模型，并将其用于伟大的事情和可怕的事情，”AI 安全公司 Alice 的首席执行官诺姆·施瓦茨 (Noam Schwartz) 表示，该公司为 AI 模型开发人员进行了红队和安全评估。

contentParagraphs.en: Teaching models when to say "no"
contentParagraphs.zh: 教导何时说“不”

contentParagraphs.en: Big AI companies such as OpenAI, Google, Anthropic and xAI train their proprietary models to refuse requests deemed as harmful or inappropriate. Legions of workers instruct models when and how to refuse certain prompts.
contentParagraphs.zh: OpenAI、Google、Anthropic 和 xAI 等大型人工智能公司训练其专有模型来拒绝被视为有害或不适当的请求。大批工作人员指导模特何时以及如何拒绝某些提示。

contentParagraphs.en: These methods don't always work and carry pitfalls: some harmful requests go through, while other users complain about innocuous requests being refused. Chatbots that initially say "no" can be manipulated into saying "yes" using cleverly phrased prompts, such as posing them as poems. Even with guardrails, popular chatbots have been used to plan mass violence and generate deepfake child sexual abuse material. In some instances, parents have accused AI chatbots of encouraging their children to harm themselves.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些方法并不总是有效，并且存在陷阱：一些有害的请求会被通过，而其他用户则抱怨无害的请求被拒绝。最初说“不”的聊天机器人可以通过巧妙的措辞提示（例如将它们摆成诗歌）来操纵说“是”。即使有护栏，流行的聊天机器人也被用来策划大规模暴力并生成深度伪造的儿童性虐待材料。在某些情况下，家长指责人工智能聊天机器人鼓励孩子伤害自己。

contentParagraphs.en: But there's a whole other class of AI models whose guardrails are much easier to strip away. They're known as open-weight models. Some are made by tech giants, such as OpenAI and Alibaba, while others are put out by smaller outfits like China's DeepSeek. Like their better-known proprietary counterparts, many possess advanced capabilities such as writing functional code or generating life-like images. Unlike with ChatGPT, Claude or Gemini, it's easier to permanently remove their built-in safety guardrails – and the companies behind them have no idea how they're being used.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但还有一类人工智能模型，其护栏更容易被拆除。它们被称为开放重量模型。其中一些是由 OpenAI 和阿里巴巴等科技巨头开发的，而另一些则是由中国 DeepSeek 等规模较小的公司推出的。与更知名的专有同行一样，许多拥有高级功能，例如编写功能代码或生成栩栩如生的图像。与 ChatGPT、Claude 或 Gemini 不同，永久移除它们内置的安全护栏更容易——而且它们背后的公司不知道它们是如何使用的。

contentParagraphs.en: Getting rid of open-weight models' guardrails used to take time and deep expertise. But in recent months, that process has become dramatically more accessible and popular.
contentParagraphs.zh: 过去，摆脱开放重量模型的护栏需要时间和深厚的专业知识。但近几个月来，这一过程变得更加容易获得和流行。

contentParagraphs.en: Recent method makes removing model guardrails easier than ever
contentParagraphs.zh: 最近的方法使拆除模型护栏比以往更容易

contentParagraphs.en: Safety guardrails of open-weight models can be weakened or removed in many ways. This is largely because the model developers have made what's known as the model weights available to the public. Model weights are sets of parameters, like knobs and dials in a machine, telling the models how to process information.
contentParagraphs.zh: 开放式重量模型的安全护栏可以通过多种方式削弱或移除。这主要是因为模型开发人员向公众公开了所谓的模型权重。模型权重是一组参数，例如机器中的旋钮和转盘，告诉模型如何处理信息。

contentParagraphs.en: One recently developed method called "abliteration" has caught the attention of AI and national security researchers. By tweaking model weights, people can take away the model's ability to say "no."
contentParagraphs.zh: 最近开发的一种名为“消除”的方法引起了人工智能和国家安全研究人员的注意。通过调整模型权重，人们可以消除模型说“不”的能力。

contentParagraphs.en: Hugging Face, which hosts open-source AI models, currently lists over 6,000 abliterated models, compared to about 600 in 2024. On Hugging Face, abliterated models currently outnumber models that have their guardrails removed using other methods, according to research by the National Counterterrorism Innovation, Technology, and Education Center (NCITE), a Department of Homeland Security-supported research consortium based at University of Nebraska at Omaha.
contentParagraphs.zh: 托管开源人工智能模型的 Hugging Face 目前列出了 6,000 多个被删减的模型，而 2024 年大约有 600 个。根据国家反恐创新、技术和教育中心 (NCITE) 的研究，在 Hugging Face 上，被删减的模型目前超过了使用其他方法移除护栏的模型。NCITE 是一个由国土安全部支持的研究联盟，总部设在奥马哈内布拉斯加大学。

contentParagraphs.en: What's more, new tools are making it much easier to create abliterated models. "That was [the job of] the data scientist, you know, a senior employee" at a leading AI lab, said Schwartz. "Now, everybody with access to the internet and a laptop for like 400 bucks can actually run this thing on their own machine."
contentParagraphs.zh: 更重要的是，新工具使创建删减模型变得更加容易。施瓦茨说，“这是数据科学家的工作，你知道，他是一家领先人工智能实验室的高级员工”。 “现在，每个能够访问互联网并拥有 400 美元笔记本电脑的人实际上都可以在自己的机器上运行这个东西。”

contentParagraphs.en: One such tool is Heretic, which automates the abliteration process. All a user has to do to remove a model's guardrails is to give Heretic two lines of instructions, and the process can take as little as a few minutes. The application has gotten more popular on the code repository GitHub since February, according to Alice's research.
contentParagraphs.zh: Heretic 就是这样的工具之一，它可以自动执行消除过程。用户只需向 Heretic 发出两行指令即可移除模型的护栏，整个过程只需几分钟。根据 Alice 的研究，自 2 月份以来，该应用程序在代码存储库 GitHub 上变得越来越受欢迎。

contentParagraphs.en: Some lawmakers are taking notice. In late April, House lawmakers attended a demonstration of abliterated models hosted by NCITE, Politico reported.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些立法者正在注意到这一点。据 Politico 报道，4 月下旬，众议院议员参加了 NCITE 主办的废除模型示威活动。

contentParagraphs.en: "[What] was frightening about this demonstration was how readily available some of this content or software is on kind of the black market right now, and how it can be weaponized and used to manipulate people, destroy lives and build weapons of mass destruction," said Rep. Andy Ogles (R-TN) in a video put out by Republicans on the House Homeland Security Committee.
contentParagraphs.zh: 众议员安迪·奥格尔斯 (Andy Ogles) 在众议院国土安全委员会共和党人发布的视频中表示，“这次示威活动的可怕之处在于，这些内容或软件现在在黑市上很容易获得，以及它们如何被武器化并用于操纵人民、摧毁生命和制造大规模杀伤性武器。”

contentParagraphs.en: Models without guardrails can be both useful and dangerous
contentParagraphs.zh: 没有护栏的模型既有用又危险

contentParagraphs.en: It is difficult to get a comprehensive picture of how people are using open-weight models, because these models are run locally on users' computers, and don't need the internet to function. Unlike with proprietary models, the model developers cannot monitor what users are asking the models.
contentParagraphs.zh: 很难全面了解人们如何使用开放权重模型，因为这些模型在用户计算机上本地运行，不需要互联网即可运行。与专有模型不同，模型开发人员无法监控用户对模型的询问。

contentParagraphs.en: But there's growing anecdotal evidence for how people are experimenting with altered models.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但越来越多的轶事证据表明人们正在尝试改变模型。

contentParagraphs.en: Several accounts on X said they have used abliterated models to generate pornography.
contentParagraphs.zh: X 上的几个帐户表示，他们使用删减模型来生成色情内容。

contentParagraphs.en: An individual in a pro-ISIS chat room claimed they used an "uncensored" AI to research the amount and type of explosives needed to destroy "Trump Tower in the U.S.," according to the Counter Extremism Project, a nonprofit that focuses on counterterrorism.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据专注于反恐的非营利组织反极端主义项目称，支持伊斯兰国的聊天室中的一个人声称他们使用“未经审查的”人工智能来研究摧毁“美国特朗普大厦”所需的爆炸物的数量和类型。

contentParagraphs.en: On one cybercrime forum, a user asked for ideas to get around an AI model's guardrails so they could use AI to make scam calls. Another user recommended Heretic, according to research by Alice.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在一个网络犯罪论坛上，一位用户询问如何绕过人工智能模型的护栏，以便他们可以使用人工智能拨打诈骗电话。根据爱丽丝的研究，另一位用户推荐了 Heretic。

contentParagraphs.en: While giving users information
contentParagraphs.zh: 在向用户提供信息的同时

articles.title: Anthropic’s alliance with pope on AI harms: all in good faith or ‘Vatican-washing?’
articles.summary:
Experts say AI firm’s engagement with Vatican risks creating ‘feelgood’ discourse that lacks critical examination
Why did Anthropic’s founder sit beside the pope during a warning about AI?
In the first major written teaching of his papacy, Pope Leo XIV took artificial intelligenc
articles.titleZh: Anthropic 与教皇在人工智能问题上的联盟有害：一切都是出于善意还是“洗教梵蒂冈”？
articles.summaryZh:
专家表示，人工智能公司与梵蒂冈的接触可能会产生缺乏严格审查的“感觉良好”的话语
为什么 Anthropic 的创始人在关于人工智能的警告期间坐在教皇旁边？
教皇利奥十四世在其教皇任期内的第一个主要书面教学中，采用了人工智能

contentParagraphs.en: Why did Anthropic’s founder sit beside the pope during a warning about AI?In the first major written teaching of his papacy, Pope Leo XIV took artificial intelligence to task. The pontiff delineated the technology’s most concerning threats to humanity: replacing workers, accelerating war and exploiting the environment. At a ceremony honoring the holy teaching the day of its release at the Vatican, the pope was flanked by an unusual guest speaker: Anthropic co-founder Chris Olah, one of the people behind the AI boom so worrying Leo.Olah’s presence raises a key question: how could the Catholic church and the world’s most valuable AI startup work together, when Anthropic’s technology may bring about the future Leo is warning against?Leo’s encyclical discusses at length the preservation of the dignity of humans’ work as it comes under threat from AI – but major AI companies, including Anthropic, aren’t prioritising these concerns, says Pete Furlong, senior manager of policy and research at Center for Humane Technology, a nonprofit advocating for accountability around AI.“All of these companies are building technology that … is designed to replace people,” Furlong says. “That’s very much at odds with the pope’s words. You can’t have dignity in a world where you’re building technology to replace people.”Some professions – such as coders, customer service representatives and data-entry workers – are especially vulnerable to AI’s ability to automate tasks, according to Anthropic’s own labor market analysis released in March. A survey published by nonprofit AI research center Epoch AI last month found that 20% of full-time workers in the US said AI has taken over parts of their job. Dario Amodei himself, CEO of Anthropic, has warned of an apocalyptic loss of white-collar jobs in the coming years.‘Vatican-washing’There’s a risk that Anthropic’s engagement with the Vatican could remain superficial and lead to a “feelgood” discourse without critical self-examination, for both sides, says Paolo Carozza, a law professor at Notre Dame law school and co-chair of the Meta Oversight Board. “This is Anthropic’s brand, right? That’s how they’re distinguishing themselves, by aligning themselves with the more safety and responsibility oriented voices. There’s something to be gained by saying, ‘Look, even the pope is willing to talk to us because of [our pro-safety brand]. Google wasn’t on the stage and OpenAI wasn’t on the stage,’” Carozza says.Carozza remains hopeful – even though he was initially skeptical about Olah’s presence at the ceremony. “There has to be dialogue among all of the actors here, and it can’t be an us-versus-them thing,” he added.Furlong, at the Center for Humane Technology, largely agrees. “What the pope’s writing says is in conflict with what Anthropic says. To me, that’s a good sign,” he says. Furlong believes it’s worth taking Anthropic’s efforts at face value, for now, and that it’s important to engage AI’s pioneers, while being cautious about how increasing financial pressures like going public may bend their future stances.Olah noted in his remarks that every frontier AI lab “operates inside a set of incentives and constraints that can sometimes conflict with doing the right thing”.“No matter how sincerely any of us intend to do the right thing – and I believe many of us do – we will always be influenced by those incentives,” he said.Some AI safety advocates feel the pope was not forceful enough in trying to rein in AI’s harms. Timnit Gebru, founder of the Distributed Artificial Intelligence Research Institute, wrote in a Linkedin post that the alliance was effectively “Vatican-washing” and said the church should have partnered with “the exploited data workers fighting for their rights, the people whose water is polluted fighting data centers, or the many other victims around the world”.Anthropic did not offer comment on the matter.Anthropic and Leo align on concerns over AI and warThe church and Anthropic do agree on other issues, such as red lines on using AI in warfare. Leo wrote about how AI can “lower the threshold for the use of force, shield people from responsibility and foster a culture in which the enemy is reduced to a statistic and the victim to ‘collateral damage’.” He called for the “most rigorous ethical constraints” to protect “the sanctity of life and to avoid a race to develop such arms.”When Amodei refused to allow the US government to use his company’s AI models in fully autonomous weapons and mass surveillance earlier this year, it led to a bitter feud with the president. The Trump administration subsequently blacklisted Anthropic, and designated the AI company as a supply chain threat – prompting a court battle, which is ongoing.Anthropic has aligned its brand with being pro-AI safety in contrast to its competitor OpenAI, where Amodei once worked, by acknowledging the risks of its AI systems, and promoting safeguards for responsible AI use. The company spent a record $1.6m on lobbying in the first quarter of 2026 – beating out competitor OpenAI. Much of their advocacy in Washington and state legislatures promotes AI regulation.Leo calls for more sustainable data centers while Anthropic keeps buildingTucked away in one paragraph of the roughly 42,000-word encyclical is a soft critique of the datacenters powering the AI boom, and a call to reduce their environmental damage. “Current AI systems require enormous amounts of energy and water, significantly influencing carbon dioxide emissions, and place heavy demands on natural resources,” Leo wrote. “For this reason, it is essential to develop more sustainable technological solutions that reduce environmental impact and help protect our common home.”Datacenters have sparked nationwide backlash in the US, which is home to the greatest number of them in the world, from communities worried about negative effects, from industrial emissions to rocketing energy bills. These energy-guzzling computer clusters are foundati

articles.title: Ferrari wanted to take on Chinese EVs with the Luce - then the backlash started
articles.summary: The brand's first EV has been heavily criticised, with some saying it has abandoned Ferrari's roots.
articles.titleZh: 法拉利希望通过 Luce 来挑战中国电动汽车 - 然后强烈反对就开始了
articles.summaryZh: 该品牌的首款电动汽车受到了严厉批评，有人说它放弃了法拉利的根源。

articles.title: Reality TV can be unserious and dramatic. It's also training political candidates
articles.summary: Reality shows are an escape with characters who can be larger than life. But some stars use the experience of fame as a stepping stone toward another challenge: running for political office.
articles.titleZh: 电视真人秀可能不严肃且戏剧性。它还在培训政治候选人
articles.summaryZh: 真人秀是一种逃避现实的方式，其中的人物可能比生活更伟大。但一些明星利用成名经历作为迈向另一个挑战的垫脚石：竞选政治职位。

contentParagraphs.en: Luke Gulbranson is running for Congress as a Democrat in Minnesota's 8th Congressional District. But he may be best known for appearing in reality television before entering politics.
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢克·古尔布兰森 (Luke Gulbranson) 正在明尼苏达州第八国会选区作为民主党人竞选国会议员。但他最出名的可能是在进入政坛之前出现在电视真人秀节目中。

contentParagraphs.en: Luke Gulbranson for Congress
contentParagraphs.zh: 国会议员卢克·古尔布兰森

contentParagraphs.en: Luke Gulbranson for Congress
contentParagraphs.zh: 国会议员卢克·古尔布兰森

contentParagraphs.en: Luke Gulbranson had just gotten off the phone with his parents, was sipping his morning coffee, reciting his daily prayers and watching President Trump speak with reporters when he had a realization: he was going to run for Congress.
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢克·古尔布兰森刚刚和父母通完电话，一边喝着早晨的咖啡，背诵着每日祈祷，一边看着特朗普总统与记者交谈，这时他意识到：他要竞选国会议员。

contentParagraphs.en: "In that moment I was like, 'Wait, I'm going to do this. I'm going to actually throw my hat in the ring and do this,'" Gulbranson told NPR about his decision to challenge Republican Rep. Peter Stauber in Minnesota's 8th Congressional District as a Democrat.
contentParagraphs.zh: “那一刻我想，‘等等，我要做这件事。我真的要投身于这件事，’”古尔布兰森告诉 NPR，他决定以民主党人的身份在明尼苏达州第八国会区挑战共和党众议员彼得·斯托伯 (Peter Stauber)。

contentParagraphs.en: Gulbranson is a political newcomer. This is his first campaign for elected office. But he's no stranger to life in the public eye. Before he entered the race, millions watched Gulbranson for three seasons on Bravo's reality television series "Summer House" and two seasons of its spin-off "Winter House."
contentParagraphs.zh: 古尔布兰森是一位政治新人。这是他第一次竞选公职。但他对公众眼中的生活并不陌生。在参加竞选之前，数百万人观看了古尔布兰森的布拉沃真人秀电视连续剧《夏宫》的三季及其衍生剧《冬宫》的两季。

contentParagraphs.en: Bravo TV star Luke Gulbranson attends Supermodels Unlimited Magazine's Cover Release Party during New York Fashion Week on February 12 in New York City.
contentParagraphs.zh: Bravo 电视明星 Luke Gulbranson 于 2 月 12 日在纽约时装周期间出席 Supermodels Unlimited 杂志的封面发布派对。

contentParagraphs.en: He isn't the only former reality TV star making a political debut in 2026 — at least two others are hopping into the political arena. Farrah Abraham, former star of MTV's "Teen Mom," announced she is running for Austin City Council, though she has not filed the necessary paperwork to officially run. And perhaps most well-known this election cycle, Spencer Pratt, the "villain" of MTV's early 2000s hit show "The Hills" is vying to become the next mayor of Los Angeles. While some people may consider reality shows unserious, dramatic and even trashy, the genre seems to have become a training ground for politics.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他并不是唯一一位在 2026 年首次亮相政治的前真人秀明星——至少还有另外两人正在跳入政治舞台。 MTV 节目《青少年妈妈》的前明星法拉·亚伯拉罕 (Farrah Abraham) 宣布，她将竞选奥斯汀市议会议员，但她尚未提交正式竞选所需的文件。也许本次选举周期中最著名的是 MTV 2000 年代初热门节目《The Hills》中的“恶棍”斯宾塞·普拉特 (Spencer Pratt)，他正在争夺洛杉矶下一任市长的职位。虽然有些人可能认为真人秀节目不严肃、戏剧性甚至垃圾，但这种类型似乎已经成为政治的训练场。

contentParagraphs.en: David Bresenham is an executive producer of reality TV Shows. Bresenham — who is also a lecturer at Stanford University where he teaches a class about reality shows and society — believes reality TV stars have what it takes to withstand the metaphorical bloodbath that politics can become.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大卫·布雷森汉姆 (David Bresenham) 是真人秀节目的执行制片人。布雷森纳姆也是斯坦福大学的讲师，他在那里教授真人秀和社会课程。他相信真人秀明星有能力承受政治可能发生的隐喻性大屠杀。

contentParagraphs.en: These stars know how to navigate conflict, work a camera and often have experience dealing with public backlash. While traditional politicians are figuring out how to handle criticism, build name recognition and reach audiences through newer mediums, like social media, reality tv stars already have those credentials.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些明星知道如何处理冲突、如何使用相机，并且经常有处理公众强烈反对的经验。虽然传统政客正在研究如何应对批评、建立知名度并通过社交媒体等新媒体接触观众，但真人秀明星已经具备了这些资格。

contentParagraphs.en: "Politics, certainly today, you need to be able to interact well with cameras. You need to be able to speak in soundbites. And you need to be able to present your ideas as succinctly as possible," Bresenham said. "If you've had success in reality TV, you're probably pretty good at those."
contentParagraphs.zh: “当然，今天的政治，你需要能够与摄像机进行良好的互动。你需要能够用原声说话。你需要能够尽可能简洁地表达你的想法，”布雷森汉姆说。 “如果你在真人秀节目中取得了成功，那么你可能很擅长这些。”

contentParagraphs.en: Gulbranson doesn't see his time on television as an advantage against his opponents but he does believe it prepared him for politics in a way. In his view, politics isn't for the faint of heart and neither is being cast on a reality show.
contentParagraphs.zh: 古尔布兰森并不认为他在电视上的时间比他的对手有优势，但他确实相信这在某种程度上为他从政做好了准备。在他看来，政治不适合胆小的人，真人秀也不适合。

contentParagraphs.en: "I definitely think it helps me in having thick skin because I've noticed that politics is actually worse than reality television, " Gulbranson said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我确实认为这对我脸皮厚有帮助，因为我注意到政治实际上比电视真人秀更糟糕，”古尔布兰森说。

contentParagraphs.en: The characters on the screen are, by nature of the genre, constantly and viciously judged by a distant audience; everyone who watches has an opinion about what is unfolding in the latest episode and it impacts how the public perceives a person. On "Summer House," Gulbranson was known as the small town, dreamy guy with a reputation of toying with the emotions of some of his female housemates and being cast in that role can be difficult. Still, Gulbranson lives beyond the edit.
contentParagraphs.zh: 由于该类型的本质，银幕上的角色经常受到远处观众的恶意评判。每个观看的人都对最新剧集中发生的事情有自己的看法，这会影响公众对一个人的看法。在《夏日别墅》中，古尔布兰森被称为小镇上的梦想家，以玩弄一些女室友的情感而闻名，要扮演这个角色可能很困难。 Still, Gulbranson lives beyond the edit.

contentParagraphs.en: "I'm confident in who I am as a man and as I continue to meet people on the campaign trail, they see that, too," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说：“我对自己作为一个男人充满信心，当我继续在竞选过程中与人们见面时，他们也看到了这一点。”

contentParagraphs.en: After several seasons on Bravo's reality programs "Summer House" and "Winter House," Luke Gulbranson moved back to Minnesota where he runs a maple syrup business and coaches hockey.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在布拉沃的真人秀节目“夏宫”和“冬宫”中演出了几个赛季后，卢克·古尔布兰森搬回明尼苏达州，在那里经营枫糖浆生意并担任曲棍球教练。

contentParagraphs.en: Luke Gulbranson for Congress
contentParagraphs.zh: 国会议员卢克·古尔布兰森

contentParagraphs.en: Luke Gulbranson for Congress
contentParagraphs.zh: 国会议员卢克·古尔布兰森

contentParagraphs.en: After his time on TV, Gulbranson moved back to his hometown of Eveleth, Minn., where he manages his own maple syrup business and coaches hockey.
contentParagraphs.zh: 结束电视生涯后，古尔布兰森搬回家乡明尼苏达州埃弗莱斯，在那里管理自己的枫糖浆业务并担任曲棍球教练。

contentParagraphs.en: Instead of trying to connect with constituents through the parasocial relationship created by television, he's leaning into his upbringing.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他没有试图通过电视创造的准社会关系与选民建立联系，而是倾向于自己的成长经历。

contentParagraphs.en: Gulbranson describes himself as a "welfare kid" who grew up on powdered milk and food stamps. Now, at 42-years-old, he said he is noticing that same struggle in the community he grew up in and across Northern Minnesota. His parents, who he said should be retired by now, still work because they can't afford not to. Gulbranson's mom has leukemia and his dad is disabled with diabetes. He's not worried about his reality TV persona. He said he's worried about affordability, the shrinking middle class, the loss of union jobs and access to child care and healthcare.
contentParagraphs.zh: 古尔布兰森将自己描述为一个靠奶粉和食品券长大的“福利孩子”。现在，42 岁的他说，他注意到在明尼苏达州北部和整个他长大的社区中也存在着同样的斗争。他说他的父母现在应该退休了，但他们仍在工作，因为他们承担不起不工作的后果。古尔布兰森的母亲患有白血病，他的父亲患有糖尿病而致残。他并不担心自己在真人秀节目中的形象。他说，他担心负担能力、中产阶级萎缩、工会工作岗位的流失以及儿童保育和医疗保健的机会。

contentParagraphs.en: "In reality television, it affects me. It affects the way I'm perceived on how I'm edited and produced and stuff. But with Congress, it affects the lives of other people. You can watch a TV show and you might care about it, but it's not affecting your health care, right? It's not affecting the cost of goods or every time you're putting gas in the car," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “在电视真人秀中，它会影响我。它会影响人们对我的剪辑、制作等方式的看法。但在国会，它会影响其他人的生活。你可以看电视节目，你可能会关心它，但这不会影响你的医疗保健，对吧？它不会影响商品成本或每次给汽车加油的时间，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: Bresenham, the reality show executive producer and lecturer at Stanford, said for all of the genre's faults, it has placed a great amount of social currency on being reliable, authentic and shameless. Even if the person on the other side of the screen disagrees with their position, there is usually a mutual respect for the star standing in their convictions.
contentParagraphs.zh: 真人秀节目执行制片人兼斯坦福大学讲师布雷森纳姆表示，尽管该类型节目存在种种缺陷，但它已经将大量的社会货币放在了可靠、真实和无耻上。即使屏幕另一边的人不同意他们的立场，他们通常也会相互尊重坚持自己信念的明星。

contentParagraphs.en: Enter Spencer Pratt.
contentParagraphs.zh: 斯宾塞·普拉特登场。

contentParagraphs.en: Spencer Pratt appears on "Fox & Friends" at Fox News Channel Studios on May 28 in New York City.
contentParagraphs.zh: 斯宾塞·普拉特 (Spencer Pratt) 于 5 月 28 日出现在纽约福克斯新闻频道工作室的“福克斯与朋友”节目中。

articles.title: Israel issues more displacement orders in Lebanon, seizes strategic castle
articles.summary: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz describes the capture of Beaufort Castle as a significant tactical victory.
articles.titleZh: 以色列在黎巴嫩发布更多流离失所令，夺取战略城堡
articles.summaryZh: 以色列国防部长伊斯雷尔·卡茨将夺取博福特城堡描述为一次重大的战术胜利。

contentParagraphs.en: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz describes the capture of Beaufort Castle as a significant tactical victory.Israel has ordered the forced displacement of all residents living south of the Zahrani River in southern Lebanon as it expands its invasion of the country before a possible assault on the city of Nabatieh.In a post on the social media platform X on Sunday, Israeli military Arabic spokesperson Avichay Adraee instructed residents to move immediately north of the Zahrani River, warning that anyone who remained in the area risked being killed.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Harvard Medical School graduate dedicates speech to Lebanon and Palestinelist 2 of 3Netanyahu says Israeli forces have crossed Lebanon’s Litani Riverlist 3 of 3Israeli soldiers reach Nabatieh, one of southern Lebanon’s biggest citiesend of listIsrael had issued more than 10 displacement orders in the previous 24 hours as it expanded its offensive against Hezbollah, after its forces crossed the Litani River on Friday for the first time since 2006.Israel seized Beaufort Castle, which sits atop a strategic hill near Nabatieh, Lebanon’s fifth largest city. The Israeli military previously captured the 12th-century fortress in 1982 and maintained control of it until withdrawing from Lebanon 18 years later.Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz described the capture as a significant tactical victory.“Under Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and my direction, the [Israeli military] expanded the operations in Lebanon, crossed the Litani River, and captured the Beaufort Ridge – one of the most important strategic points for defending the communities of the Galilee and safeguarding the security of our forces.”Israel has been encircling Nabatieh, a city that is key to southern Lebanon’s economy and a cultural hub for the region.Nabatieh is viewed by many Lebanese as a symbol of resistance due to its historic role on the front line of Israeli assaults.Despite Israel’s advances in southern Lebanon, Hezbollah, which is backed by Iran, has continued to target Israeli forces. On Sunday, Israel confirmed that one of its soldiers was killed in a Hezbollah drone attack a day earlier, bringing the total number of Israeli soldiers killed since the current round of fighting escalated on March 2 to 25.Al Jazeera correspondent Nida Ibrahim, reporting from the occupied West Bank, said the Israeli government believes Hezbollah has strengthened in recent months and is now showing greater defiance, particularly through the use of drones that have caused casualties in Israel.Hezbollah has been deploying fibre-optic drones that are resistant to electronic jamming and near-impossible to detect by radar. The low-cost aircraft have been able to penetrate Israel’s multi-layered air defence systems, which are valued at billions of dollars.‘Scorched-earth policy’Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam has denounced Israel’s invasion of the country, accusing its southern neighbour of pursuing a “scorched-earth policy” and imposing “collective punishment” on residents of southern Lebanon. Israel also has repeatedly been accused of collective punishment against Palestinians in Gaza over its genocidal war and blockade of the enclave, including severe restrictions on the flow of humanitarian aid.Reporting from the southern city of Tyre, Al Jazeera’s Obaida Hitto said Israel’s invasion had caused a humanitarian crisis.“The Israeli military has created a zone that is essentially one-fifth of Lebanon’s territory where Lebanese citizens can’t go. Many families can’t return to their homes. There’s ongoing demolitions. Today, Israeli troops continued [to] demolish homes and businesses.”Israel and Lebanon are currently engaged in peace talks facilitated by the United States. Officials from both countries met at the Pentagon in Washington, DC, on Friday to discuss the implementation of the nominal “ceasefire” that came into effect in mid-April, which Israel has repeatedly violated. The negotiations are expected to resume next week.Filippo Dionigi, Senior Lecturer in International Relations at the University of Bristol, told Al Jazeera that the Lebanese government is in an extremely difficult position because Israel is not currently interested in peace, which strengthens Hezbollah’s narrative that it is the country’s only means of defence. Dionigi added that Hezbollah’s opposition to the peace talks further adds to the pressure.“On one hand they have to negotiate with Israel and Israel fundamentally is indicating that it has no significant political plan for Lebanon, it is basically pursuing only a military strategy at this point. On the other hand, they’re [also] pressured from the domestic political scene.”Both Lebanon and Israel want to see Hezbollah disarmed, but Beirut has struggled with the task. Hezbollah, which is stronger than the official state army, argues that it is the country’s only means of defence against Israel.
contentParagraphs.zh: 以色列国防部长伊斯雷尔·卡茨将夺取博福特城堡描述为一次重大的战术胜利。以色列已下令强迫居住在黎巴嫩南部扎赫拉尼河以南的所有居民流离失所，因为它在可能袭击纳巴蒂耶市之前扩大了对该国的入侵。周日，以色列军方阿拉伯语发言人阿维凯·阿德拉伊在社交媒体平台X上发帖，指示居民立即迁移到扎赫拉尼河以北，警告留在该地区的任何人都有被杀害的危险。 list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3哈佛大学医学院毕业生向黎巴嫩和巴勒斯坦发表演讲 2 of 3内塔尼亚胡称以色列军队已越过黎巴嫩的利塔尼河list 3 of 3以色列士兵抵达黎巴嫩南部最大的城市之一纳巴蒂耶end of list以色列在军队越境后扩大了对真主党的攻势，在过去24小时内发布了10多项流离失所令周五，这是自 2006 年以来的首次袭击利塔尼河。以色列占领了博福特城堡，该城堡位于黎巴嫩第五大城市纳巴提耶附近的一座战略山顶上。以色列军队曾于 1982 年占领了这座 12 世纪的堡垒，并一直控制着它，直到 18 年后从黎巴嫩撤军。以色列国防部长以色列·卡茨将这次占领描述为一次重大的战术胜利。“在本杰明·内塔尼亚胡总理和我的指导下，[以色列军队]扩大了在黎巴嫩的行动，跨过利塔尼河，占领了博福特岭——这是保卫社区的最重要的战略要点之一以色列一直在包围纳巴提耶，这座城市是黎巴嫩南部经济的关键城市，也是该地区的文化中心。由于纳巴提耶在以色列袭击前线发挥的历史作用，许多黎巴嫩人将纳巴提耶视为抵抗的象征。尽管以色列在黎巴嫩南部取得进展，但得到伊朗支持的真主党仍继续以以色列军队为目标。周日，以色列证实，一名士兵在一天前的真主党无人机袭击中丧生，这使得本轮战斗自3月2日升级至25日以来阵亡的以色列士兵总数达到了新高。半岛电视台记者尼达·易卜拉欣在被占领的约旦河西岸报道称，以色列政府认为真主党近几个月来有所加强，现在表现出更大的反抗，特别是通过使用无人机在以色列造成人员伤亡。真主党一直在部署光纤无人机能够抵抗电子干扰并且几乎不可能被雷达探测到。这些低成本飞机已经能够突破以色列价值数十亿美元的多层防空系统。 “焦土政策”黎巴嫩总理纳瓦夫·萨拉姆谴责以色列入侵该国，指责其南部邻国奉行“焦土政策”并对黎巴嫩南部居民实施“集体惩罚”。以色列还多次被指责对加沙的巴勒斯坦人进行种族灭绝战争和封锁飞地，包括严格限制人道主义援助的流动。半岛电视台记者奥贝达·希托从南部城市提尔报道称，以色列的入侵造成了人道主义危机。“以色列军队创建了一个基本上占黎巴嫩领土五分之一的区域，黎巴嫩公民无法前往该区域。许多家庭无法返回家园。”今天，以色列军队继续拆除房屋和企业。”以色列和黎巴嫩目前正在美国的推动下进行和平谈判。两国官员周五在华盛顿五角大楼会面，讨论四月中旬生效的名义“停火”的实施情况，以色列一再违反这一停火协议。谈判预计下周恢复。 布里斯托大学国际关系高级讲师菲利波·迪奥尼吉告诉半岛电视台，黎巴嫩政府处于极其困难的境地，因为以色列目前对和平不感兴趣，这强化了真主党的说法，即以色列是该国唯一的防御手段。迪奥尼吉补充说，真主党反对和谈进一步增加了压力。“一方面，他们必须与以色列谈判，而以色列从根本上表明它对黎巴嫩没有重大政治计划，目前基本上只追求军事战略。另一方面，他们也受到国内政治舞台的压力。”黎巴嫩和以色列都希望看到真主党解除武装，但贝鲁特一直在努力完成这项任务。比官方国家军队更强大的真主党声称，这是该国抵御以色列的唯一手段。

articles.title: The strait may reopen, but global confidence may not return
articles.summary: The next phase of the Strait of Hormuz crisis may be defined less by its closure than by conditional access.
articles.titleZh: 海峡可能重新开放，但全球信心可能不会恢复
articles.summaryZh: 霍尔木兹海峡危机的下一阶段可能不是由其关闭来定义，而是由有条件的准入来定义。

contentParagraphs.en: United States President Donald Trump’s claim that a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz has been largely negotiated may calm markets temporarily. But the deeper significance of the current crisis lies elsewhere. The issue is no longer only whether trade routes remain open but who has the power to condition access to them.The specific terms of any agreement may evolve, and any diplomatic arrangement may still be delayed, contested or revised. But the broader pattern is already visible: Strategic trade routes are becoming more politically managed, commercially exposed and geopolitically contested.The danger is not necessarily that diplomacy fails. The more important risk is that it succeeds just enough to disguise a weaker order as stability.Temporary calm is not the same as strategic stability. Calm can be negotiated; stability must be trusted.The most important shift, therefore, is not from war to peace but from disruption to governance.Iranian plans for an authority to manage the Strait of Hormuz and exert greater influence over routing decisions and possible transit tolls show that Tehran is attempting to convert temporary leverage into a more permanent role in managing the waterway.Therefore, the strategic question is shifting from access to governance. Access relates to whether ships can pass. Governance relates to who sets the rules, prices the risks, controls the exceptions and decides when normal commerce becomes conditional.This matters not only for the Gulf, but for the wider international system. States that depend heavily on maritime trade now face a situation in which commercial access is shaped not simply by markets but also by geopolitical leverage, sanctions pressure, naval power and crisis diplomacy.Asia remains central to this calculation. China, India, Japan and South Korea are among the principal end users of Gulf energy, and much of the commercial risk created by uncertainty in the Strait of Hormuz is transmitted eastwards. But the implications extend beyond Asia. Many developing economies remain highly exposed to energy volatility and shipping disruptions while possessing little influence over the geopolitical contest surrounding them.The emerging pattern suggests a world in which commerce resumes but only under temporary political conditions that must be repeatedly renegotiated. That matters because modern trade depends on more than physical access. It depends on predictability, insurance, legal clarity, naval confidence and the belief that today’s route will still be viable tomorrow.This is the difference between de-escalation and normalisation. De-escalation reduces the danger of immediate conflict. Normalisation restores confidence. At present, the first may be achievable, but the second remains distant.None of this means the Strait of Hormuz is destined for permanent crisis, nor does it mean diplomacy is futile. The point is more limited but more important: Even successful crisis management may leave behind a less reliable commercial order.For markets, this distinction is crucial. If an agreement is announced, reopening may be treated as resolution. That would be premature. Temporary calm can easily be mispriced as durable stability. Freight rates may ease, energy prices may soften and equity markets may rally. Yet none of that necessarily means the underlying risk has disappeared. It may only mean that the crisis has been deferred to the next negotiation cycle.This process has consequences well beyond oil. Refiners must plan procurement against shifting risk premiums. Manufacturers must price energy and transport volatility into their margins. Insurers must reassess exposure. Shipping firms must make routing decisions under political uncertainty. Banks and traders must account for sanctions risks, payment disruptions and compliance costs.This is how geopolitical instability enters the global economy: not only through spectacular shocks but also through recurring uncertainty that gradually raises the cost of ordinary commerce.The larger lesson of the Strait of Hormuz crisis is that globalisation is not ending. It is becoming more politically exposed and strategically conditional.Companies and governments that built their assumptions around frictionless movement must now operate in a world where passage, payments, insurance, ports and suppliers are increasingly vulnerable to geopolitical pressure. The Strait of Hormuz is only one chokepoint. But because of its centrality to global energy flows, it has become one of the clearest examples of this wider transformation.For policymakers, responding to the present crisis requires more than reassurance that ships are moving again. It requires coordination between governments, commercial operators, insurers, shipping firms and energy buyers. It also requires recognising that strategic infrastructure can no longer be treated as politically neutral.For boardrooms, the lesson is similar. Geopolitical risk can no longer sit outside procurement, logistics, treasury and insurance decisions. The question is no longer whether crises will interrupt trade. It is whether business models can absorb recurring instability without losing resilience or strategic flexibility.Whatever happens with the ongoing negotiations between Iran and the US, one thing is certain: We are unlikely to go back to the old assumption that global commerce can move through strategic chokepoints as if geopolitics were merely background noise.The views expressed in this article are the author’s own and do not necessarily reflect Al Jazeera’s editorial stance.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普声称，重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的协议已基本通过谈判达成，这可能会暂时安抚市场。但当前危机的更深层次意义还在于其他方面。问题不再只是贸易路线是否保持开放，而是谁有权限制贸易路线的通行。任何协议的具体条款都可能发生变化，任何外交安排仍可能被推迟、受到争议或修改。但更广泛的模式已经显而易见：战略贸易路线正变得更加受政治管理、商业暴露和地缘政治竞争。危险并不一定是外交失败。更重要的风险是，它的成功足以将较弱的秩序伪装成稳定。暂时的平静并不等同于战略稳定。冷静可以协商；稳定必须值得信赖。因此，最重要的转变不是从战争到和平，而是从破坏到治理。伊朗计划设立一个机构来管理霍尔木兹海峡，并对路线决策和可能的过境通行费施加更大影响，这表明德黑兰正试图将临时影响力转变为管理水道的更永久作用。因此，战略问题正在从准入转向治理。通行关系到船舶能否通过。治理涉及谁制定规则、为风险定价、控制例外情况以及决定正常商业何时成为有条件的。这不仅对海湾地区很重要，而且对更广泛的国际体系也很重要。严重依赖海上贸易的国家现在面临的情况是，商业准入不仅由市场决定，还受到地缘政治杠杆、制裁压力、海军力量和危机外交的影响。亚洲仍然是这一计算的核心。中国、印度、日本和韩国是海湾能源的主要最终用户，霍尔木兹海峡的不确定性造成的大部分商业风险都向东传播。但其影响超出了亚洲范围。许多发展中经济体仍然高度受到能源波动和航运中断的影响，而对其周围的地缘政治竞争几乎没有影响力。正在出现的模式表明，在这个世界中，商业将恢复，但只能在必须反复重新谈判的临时政治条件下进行。这很重要，因为现代贸易不仅仅依赖于物理访问。这取决于可预测性、保险、法律明确性、海军信心以及今天的路线明天仍然可行的信念。这就是降级和正常化之间的区别。降级可以减少直接冲突的危险。正常化恢复了信心。目前，第一个目标或许可以实现，但第二个目标还很遥远。这并不意味着霍尔木兹海峡注定会陷入永久性危机，也不意味着外交是徒劳的。要点虽然有限，但更重要：即使成功的危机管理也可能留下不太可靠的商业秩序。对于市场来说，这种区别至关重要。如果宣布达成协议，重新开放可能被视为解决。那还为时过早。暂时的平静很容易被误认为是持久的稳定。运费可能下降，能源价格可能走软，股市可能上涨。然而，这并不一定意味着潜在的风险已经消失。这可能只意味着危机已经推迟到下一个谈判周期。这一过程的影响远远超出了石油领域。炼油厂必须针对风险溢价的变化制定采购计划。制造商必须将能源价格和运输波动纳入利润率。保险公司必须重新评估风险敞口。航运公司必须在政治不确定性的情况下做出航线决策。银行和交易商必须考虑制裁风险、支付中断和合规成本。这就是地缘政治不稳定如何进入全球经济：不仅通过巨大的冲击，而且通过反复出现的不确定性逐渐提高普通商业的成本。霍尔木兹海峡危机更大的教训是全球化并没有结束。它变得越来越具有政治暴露性和战略条件性。围绕无摩擦运动建立假设的公司和政府现在必须在一个通道、支付、保险、港口和供应商越来越容易受到地缘政治压力的世界中运作。霍尔木兹海峡只是一个咽喉要道。但由于其在全球能源流动中的中心地位，它已成为这一更广泛转型的最明显例子之一。 对于政策制定者来说，应对当前的危机需要的不仅仅是确保船舶再次航行。它需要政府、商业运营商、保险公司、航运公司和能源买家之间的协调。它还需要认识到战略基础设施不能再被视为政治中立。对于董事会来说，教训是相似的。地缘政治风险不再存在于采购、物流、财务和保险决策之外。问题不再是危机是否会中断贸易。问题在于商业模式是否能够在不失去弹性或战略灵活性的情况下吸收反复出现的不稳定因素。无论伊朗和美国之间正在进行的谈判发生什么，有一件事是肯定的：我们不太可能回到旧的假设，即全球商业可以穿越战略瓶颈，就好像地缘政治只是背景噪音一样。本文中表达的观点仅是作者自己的观点，并不一定反映半岛电视台的编辑立场。

articles.title: Iran war day 93: Trump won’t ‘rush’ deal; Israel expands Lebanon invasion
articles.summary: Donald Trump says talks with Iran are progressing 'slowly but surely', as Israel intensifies operations in Lebanon.
articles.titleZh: 伊朗战争第93天：特朗普不会“仓促”达成协议；以色列扩大对黎巴嫩的入侵
articles.summaryZh: 唐纳德·特朗普表示，随着以色列加强在黎巴嫩的行动，与伊朗的谈判正在“缓慢但稳定地”取得进展。

contentParagraphs.en: US President Donald Trump says talks with Iran are progressing ‘slowly but surely’, as Israel intensifies operations in Lebanon.United States President Donald Trump has told Fox News he is in “no hurry” for a deal with Iran as the Israeli army claimed to have captured a strategic castle in southern Lebanon in the deepest incursion into the country in 26 years.The comments come as US media reported that Trump asked for several amendments to the preliminary agreement with Iran during his meeting at the White House on Friday.Here is the latest on the US-Iran negotiations as Israel expands its invasion of Lebanon on day 93 of the war on Iran:In Iran A senior Iranian commander warned against any further attacks on his country. “The enemy should know that any aggression against the country’s territory will be met with a response even more forceful than before,” Rear Admiral Habibollah Sayyari, the deputy army commander for coordination, said, according to Press TV. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it shot down a US drone attempting to carry out a “hostile operation” in its territorial waters, according to Iran’s semi-official Fars news agency. Fars reported that the military unveiled its newest naval attack craft, 27 Rajab, during a ceremony in Tehran’s Enqelab Square. The agency said the vessel can achieve speeds of up to 100 knots (185km/h) and is equipped to launch long-range cruise missiles. The US military said its forces “disabled” a Gambia-flagged ship attempting to sail towards an Iranian port. Iran accused the US of betraying diplomacy by continuing its naval blockade.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普表示，随着以色列加强在黎巴嫩的行动，与伊朗的谈判正在“缓慢但稳步地”取得进展。美国总统唐纳德·特朗普告诉福克斯新闻，他“不急于”与伊朗达成协议，因为以色列军队声称占领了黎巴嫩南部的一座战略城堡，这是 26 年来最深入的入侵。美国媒体报道称，特朗普周五在白宫会面时要求对与伊朗的初步协议进行几项修改。在伊朗战争第93天，以色列扩大对黎巴嫩的入侵，美伊谈判的最新进展：在伊朗，一名伊朗高级指挥官警告不要对他的国家进行任何进一步的袭击。据新闻电视台报道，负责协调的陆军副司令哈比博拉·萨亚里少将表示，“敌人应该知道，任何针对该国领土的侵略都将遭到比以前更强有力的回应。”据伊朗半官方法尔斯通讯社报道，伊斯兰革命卫队（IRGC）表示击落了一架试图在其领海进行“敌对行动”的美国无人机。法尔斯报道称，军方在德黑兰恩吉拉布广场举行的仪式上揭开了其最新海军攻击艇 27 Rajab 的面纱。该机构表示，该船的航速可达 100 节（185 公里/小时），并配备发射远程巡航导弹的能力。美国军方表示，其部队“阻止”了一艘试图驶向伊朗港口的悬挂冈比亚国旗的船只。伊朗指责美国继续实施海上封锁，背叛了外交政策。

contentParagraphs.en: War diplomacy Trump told Fox News he is in no hurry for a deal with Iran and that the US is getting what it wants “slowly but surely” in the ongoing negotiations. “The one guarantee that I have to have is that there will be no nuclear weapons,” he told his daughter-in-law, Lara Trump, in an interview on Fox News on Saturday night. The comments came as US media reported that Trump asked for several amendments to the preliminary agreement with Iran during his meeting in the White House Situation Room on Friday. The New York Times reported that Trump has “toughened the terms” of the potential framework. Axios reported that the president asked for “several amendments” to the preliminary agreement his envoys reached with Iran. Iranian state media reported on Saturday that a proposed memorandum of understanding with the US included an agreement to release $12bn in frozen assets. The report cited an “unofficial” draft of the memorandum, and a similar item carried by state TV earlier this week was dismissed by the White House as a “fabrication”. In Lebanon The Associated Press and Reuters news agencies reported that Israeli forces captured the Beaufort Castle, locally also known as Qalaat al-Shaqif, near the city of Nabatieh in southern Lebanon. The Israeli military issued a new warning, asking residents to flee south of the Zahrani River in southern Lebanon. It said it was acting forcefully against Hezbollah in the region and told all residents located south of the river to leave their homes immediately. Israeli air raids on Sunday morning targeted the southern Lebanese towns of Arnoun, Kfar Tebnit and Kfar Remman, according to our colleagues at Al Jazeera Arabic. Israeli shelling also hit the Kfarjouz area in the Nabatieh district and the town of Dbeibine in the Marjayoun district. Israel’s military spokeswoman Ella Waweya said it launched “a large-scale operation” in the Beaufort Ridge and the Wadi al-Salouqi area in southern Lebanon. The Israeli military issued a “precautionary directive” following “the identification of launches from Lebanon toward several communities in northern Israel”. The military instructed residents to take shelter, but Israeli media reported shortly afterwards that air raid sirens did not sound in the region. Lebanon’s Prime Minister Nawaf Salam accused Israel of pursuing a “scorched-earth policy” as Israeli forces expand their ground invasion and continue to push towards the city of Nabatieh.
contentParagraphs.zh: 战争外交 特朗普告诉福克斯新闻，他并不急于与伊朗达成协议，美国在正在进行的谈判中“缓慢但肯定”地得到了它想要的东西。 “我必须得到的一个保证就是不会有核武器，”他在周六晚上接受福克斯新闻采访时告诉他的儿媳劳拉·特朗普。此番言论发表之际，美国媒体报道称，特朗普周五在白宫战情室举行会议时要求对与伊朗的初步协议进行多项修改。 《纽约时报》报道称，特朗普已经“加强了潜在框架的条款”。 Axios 报道称，总统要求对其特使与伊朗达成的初步协议进行“几项修改”。伊朗官方媒体周六报道称，拟议中的与美国的谅解备忘录包括一项释放 120 亿美元冻结资产的协议。该报告引用了该备忘录的“非官方”草案，而国家电视台本周早些时候播出的类似内容被白宫斥为“捏造”。在黎巴嫩，美联社和路透社报道称，以色列军队占领了黎巴嫩南部纳巴蒂耶市附近的博福特城堡，当地也称为 Qalaat al-Shaqif。以色列军方发布新警告，要求居民逃离黎巴嫩南部扎赫拉尼河以南。声明称，正在对该地区的真主党采取强硬行动，并要求河以南的所有居民立即离开家园。据半岛电视台阿拉伯语同事透露，周日上午，以色列空袭的目标是黎巴嫩南部城镇阿尔农、卡法尔特布尼特和卡法尔雷曼。以色列炮击还袭击了纳巴蒂耶区的卡法茹兹地区和马尔贾永区的德贝宾镇。以色列军方发言人埃拉·瓦维亚表示，以色列在黎巴嫩南部的博福特岭和瓦迪萨洛奇地区发起了“大规模行动”。以色列军方在“发现从黎巴嫩向以色列北部几个社区发射导弹”后发布了一项“预防指令”。军方指示居民避难，但以色列媒体随后不久报道称，该地区没有响起空袭警报。黎巴嫩总理纳瓦夫·萨拉姆指责以色列奉行“焦土政策”，以色列军队扩大地面入侵并继续向纳巴提耶市推进。

contentParagraphs.en: In Israel Israeli forces said on Sunday they were advancing in Lebanon as part of expanded ground operations to strengthen their military position in the south of the country, where they are fighting Iran-backed Hezbollah. A significant number of Israeli ground soldiers “commenced offensive operations aimed at expanding the Forward Defense Line”, the Israeli military said in a statement, adding that the operation was expanding and forces had crossed the Litani River. The Israeli military said a 21-year-old soldier had been killed in battle in southern Lebanon on Saturday. It said four others were wounded in the incident. In the US Trump said on Saturday that the US intentionally refrained from targeting Iran’s military, arguing that past wars have shown the dangers of dismantling a country’s institutions and armed forces. He pointed to Iraq as an example of what he described as a failed US approach in past conflicts. “You look at what happened with Iraq; we did so badly, that was such a foolish thing,” he told Fox News. Congress advanced a US-Israeli military integration plan that could tie the US and Israeli militaries far more closely together, deepening their cooperation on weapons research, production and technology.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在以色列，以色列军队周日表示，他们正在黎巴嫩推进，作为扩大地面行动的一部分，以加强他们在该国南部的军事地位，他们正在那里与伊朗支持的真主党作战。以色列军方在一份声明中表示，大量以色列地面士兵“开始了旨在扩大前沿防线的进攻行动”，并补充说行动正在扩大，部队已经越过利塔尼河。以色列军方表示，周六，一名 21 岁的士兵在黎巴嫩南部的战斗中丧生。据称，事件中另有四人受伤。在美国，特朗普周六表示，美国故意不针对伊朗军队，称过去的战争已经表明了瓦解一个国家机构和武装部队的危险。他指出伊拉克是美国在过去冲突中失败做法的一个例子。 “你看看伊拉克发生的事情；我们做得太糟糕了，这是一件愚蠢的事情，”他告诉福克斯新闻。国会提出了一项美以军事一体化计划，该计划可以将美国和以色列军队更紧密地联系在一起，深化双方在武器研究、生产和技术方面的合作。

articles.title: Wembanyama, Spurs win the West, topple Thunder 111-103 in Game 7 to head to NBA Finals
articles.summary: The San Antonio Spurs celebrated after beating the odds and defeating the defending champions Oklahoma City Thunder, to advance to the NBA Finals
articles.titleZh: 温巴尼亚马带领马刺队赢得西部冠军，并在第七场比赛中以 111-103 击败雷霆队，挺进 NBA 总决赛
articles.summaryZh: 圣安东尼奥马刺队战胜卫冕冠军俄克拉荷马城雷霆队，晋级 NBA 总决赛后庆祝

contentParagraphs.en: The San Antonio Spurs celebrate after defeating the Oklahoma City Thunder in the Western Conference finals of the NBA basketball playoffs series Saturday, May 30, 2026, in Oklahoma City.
contentParagraphs.zh: 圣安东尼奥马刺队于 2026 年 5 月 30 日星期六在俄克拉荷马城举行的 NBA 篮球季后赛系列赛西部决赛中击败俄克拉荷马城雷霆队后进行庆祝。

contentParagraphs.en: Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs started the Western Conference finals with a win in Oklahoma City, then ended the series the same way.
contentParagraphs.zh: 维克多·文班亚马 (Victor Wembanyama) 和圣安东尼奥马刺队以一场在俄克拉荷马城的胜利开始了西部决赛，然后以同样的方式结束了系列赛。

contentParagraphs.en: The champions are dethroned. Wembanyama and the Spurs are headed to the NBA Finals.
contentParagraphs.zh: 冠军被废黜。温巴尼亚马和马刺队即将进入 NBA 总决赛。

contentParagraphs.en: Wembanyama scored 22 points, Julian Champagnie got 18 of his 20 off of 3-pointers and the Spurs beat the Oklahoma City Thunder 111-103 on Saturday night — bucking heavy odds to win a Game 7 on the road.
contentParagraphs.zh: 文班亚马拿下 22 分，朱利安·商帕尼三分球 20 投 18 中，周六晚上马刺队以 111-103 击败俄克拉荷马城雷霆队，在客场赢得了第七场比赛。

contentParagraphs.en: "This feeling, I can't explain it," Wembanyama said. "It's so powerful."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这种感觉，我无法解释，”温班亚马说。 “太强大了。”

contentParagraphs.en: Stephon Castle scored 16 points and De'Aaron Fox had 15. Dylan Harper added 12 and Keldon Johnson and Devin Vassell each finished with 11 for the Spurs, who are headed to the NBA Finals for the first time since 2014.
contentParagraphs.zh: 斯蒂芬·卡斯尔 (Stephon Castle) 拿下 16 分，德亚伦·福克斯 (De'Aaron Fox) 得到 15 分。迪伦·哈珀 (Dylan Harper) 贡献 12 分，凯尔登·约翰逊 (Keldon Johnson) 和德文·瓦塞尔 (Devin Vassell) 为马刺队各得 11 分，这是自 2014 年以来首次进入 NBA 总决赛。

contentParagraphs.en: They will host the New York Knicks in Game 1 on Wednesday night.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他们将在周三晚上主场迎战纽约尼克斯队的第一场比赛。

contentParagraphs.en: "Back in October, we knew we had a chance to be pretty good," Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “早在十月份，我们就知道我们有机会变得相当出色，”马刺队主教练米奇·约翰逊说道。

contentParagraphs.en: Correction — the Spurs have a chance to be great. Championship-level great.
contentParagraphs.zh: 更正——马刺队有机会变得伟大。冠军级别的太棒了。

contentParagraphs.en: A huge moment came midway through the fourth, when San Antonio's Luke Kornet blocked Oklahoma City's Isaiah Hartenstein at the rim — denying a fast-break score that would have gotten the Thunder within four.
contentParagraphs.zh: 第四节中段出现了一个重要时刻，马刺队的卢克·科内特在篮下挡住了俄克拉荷马城队的以赛亚·哈滕斯坦——阻止了雷霆队将比分追至四分的快攻得分。

contentParagraphs.en: It felt like the last gasp for the Thunder. Kornet played six minutes, missed all three of his shot attempts and finished with only two points, but the block was an epic moment.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这感觉就像是雷霆队的最后一口气。科内特打了六分钟，三投全失，最终只得到两分，但盖帽是一个史诗般的时刻。

contentParagraphs.en: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led the Thunder with 35 points and nine assists, but for the eighth consecutive season the NBA will have a new champion. Cason Wallace scored 17 points, while Jared McCain and Alex Caruso had 12 apiece for the Thunder.
contentParagraphs.zh: 谢伊·吉尔吉斯-亚历山大以 35 分和 9 次助攻领衔雷霆队，但 NBA 将连续第八个赛季迎来新的总冠军。雷霆队的卡森·华莱士拿下17分，贾里德·麦凯恩和亚历克斯·卡鲁索各得12分。

contentParagraphs.en: "You have to grow from every experience, including the tough ones," Thunder coach Mark Daigneault said. "And it's the NBA — there are tough ones. We can also be really disappointed. ... There's nobody that we don't think we can beat, respectfully."
contentParagraphs.zh: “你必须从每一次经历中成长，包括艰难的经历，”雷霆队教练马克·戴尼奥特说。 “这就是NBA——有很多艰难的事情。我们也可能会非常失望……我们认为没有人是我们无法击败的，恕我直言。”

contentParagraphs.en: After four straight games that were largely decided going into the fourth quarter — the Thunder led Game 3 by 11, the Spurs led Game 4 by 18, the Thunder led Game 5 by 10 and the Spurs led Game 6 by 26, those leads all holding up with relative ease — this one was different, worthy of a Game 7.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在连续四场比赛进入第四节后，比赛的胜负已经基本确定——雷霆队在第三场比赛中领先11分，马刺队在第四场比赛中领先18分，雷霆队在第五场比赛中领先10分，马刺队在第六场比赛中领先26分，这些领先优势都相对轻松地保持着——这一场是不同的，值得第七场比赛。

contentParagraphs.en: Spurs 80, Thunder 77 was the score going into the fourth, a bit of a back-and-forth contest where the Spurs led by as many as 14 in the first half and then by as many as 11 in the third, only to see the Thunder come roaring back both times.
contentParagraphs.zh: 马刺80，雷霆77，这是进入第四节的比分，有点像一场你来我往的较量，马刺在上半场领先14分，在第三节领先11分，结果雷霆两次都卷土重来。

contentParagraphs.en: "The players did what they've been doing all year and they met the biggest moment," Johnson said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “球员们做了他们这一整年都在做的事情，他们迎来了最重要的时刻，”约翰逊说。

contentParagraphs.en: The Spurs pulled away in the fourth again, daring the Thunder to try to come back one more time. The champions — short-handed, with Jalen Williams sidelined with a bad hamstring — just didn't have anything left.
contentParagraphs.zh: 马刺队在第四节再次拉开比分，让雷霆队再次尝试反扑。冠军队人手不足，贾伦·威廉姆斯因腿筋受伤缺阵，他们已经没有任何剩余了。

contentParagraphs.en: "Winning an NBA championship is very hard in itself to do one time," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "So to do it all over again would just only make it harder."
contentParagraphs.zh: “一次性赢得 NBA 总冠军本身就很难做到，”吉尔杰斯-亚历山大说道。 “所以重来一遍只会让事情变得更加困难。”

contentParagraphs.en: San Antonio won eight of the 12 meetings against the Thunder this season — and in the end, the only matchup that really mattered.
contentParagraphs.zh: 马刺队本赛季与雷霆队的 12 场比赛中赢了 8 场——这也是最终唯一一场真正重要的比赛。

contentParagraphs.en: "We want four more," Wembanyama said. "We're not done."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们还想要四个，”文班亚马说。 “我们还没有完成。”

articles.title: Hundreds arrested in France after wild Champions League celebrations
articles.summary: Thousands of officers tried to quell unrest as crowds of fans - some firing flares - celebrated PSG's victory over Arsenal.
articles.titleZh: 欧洲冠军联赛疯狂庆祝活动后，数百人在法国被捕
articles.summaryZh: 数千名警察试图平息骚乱，而大批球迷（其中一些还发射了照明弹）庆祝巴黎圣日耳曼战胜阿森纳。

contentParagraphs.en: Hundreds arrested in France after wild Champions League celebrations3 hours agoHarry SekulichBBC NewsClashes between football fans and police across France have led to more than 400 arrests following the victory of Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) in the Champions League final against Arsenal.Thousands of officers were deployed to curb unrest that disrupted bus, train and rail services in the capital Paris.Fireworks and flares were set off, while several police officers were injured in the fray. Police fired tear gas to disperse crowds in the city centre.There was similar violence when PSG won the same trophy last year, with celebrations turning deadly. This time, the authorities were better prepared with a "very robust, very solid system in place", according to Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez.Anadolu via Getty ImagesFlares set off in central Paris after PSG defeated ArsenalParis's iconic Champs-Élysées was swarmed by fans shortly after the French team won in a penalty shootout. Footage from the city shows flares being set off, electric bikes burning on roads and revellers smashing the glass of at least one shopfront.Earlier in the day there were clashes between police and supporters who showed up to watch the final on giant screens at PSG's Parc des Princes. Police said six vehicles, two businesses and a bus shelter were damaged during the unrest.According to the authorities. 416 had been arrested in the early hours of Sunday, including 280 people in Paris.Nuñez said seven officers had been injured and called the unrest "absolutely unacceptable". Far-right leader Marine Le Pen wrote on X: "Only in France does a football club's victory spark riots.""Only in France does everyone feel compelled to lock themselves in their homes on the evening of a victory to avoid being confronted with violence," she said.Players are due to take part in a victory parade on Sunday afternoon, which includes touring the Champ-de-Mars next to the Eiffel Tower and a reception held by French President Emmanuel Macron.AFP via Getty ImagesFlares lit up Avenue des Champs-Élysées on Saturday night.
contentParagraphs.zh: 巴黎圣日耳曼队（PSG）在冠军联赛决赛中战胜阿森纳队后，法国各地数百人被捕。哈利·塞库里奇（Harry Sekulich）英国广播公司新闻 法国各地球迷与警察之间的冲突已导致 400 多人被捕。数千名警察被部署以制止扰乱首都巴黎公共汽车、火车和铁路服务的骚乱。现场燃放了烟花和照明弹，数名警察在冲突中受伤。警方在市中心发射催泪瓦斯驱散人群。去年巴黎圣日耳曼赢得同一座奖杯时也发生了类似的暴力事件，庆祝活动变得致命。内政部长洛朗·努涅斯 (Laurent Nuñez) 表示，这一次，当局做好了更充分的准备，建立了“非常强大、非常可靠的系统”。巴黎圣日耳曼击败阿森纳后，阿纳多卢 (Anadolu via Getty Images) 在巴黎市中心出发。法国队在点球大战中获胜后不久，巴黎标志性的香榭丽舍大街就挤满了球迷。来自该市的视频显示，有人点燃照明弹，电动自行车在道路上燃烧，狂欢者砸碎了至少一家店面的玻璃。当天早些时候，警察和在巴黎圣日耳曼王子公园球场大屏幕上观看决赛的支持者之间发生了冲突。警方称，骚乱期间有六辆车、两家企业和一个公交车候车亭遭到损坏。据当局称。周日凌晨，已有 416 人被捕，其中巴黎有 280 人。努涅斯表示，有 7 名警察受伤，并称骚乱“绝对不可接受”。极右翼领导人马琳·勒庞在X上写道：“只有在法国，足球俱乐部的胜利才会引发骚乱。”她说：“只有在法国，每个人都觉得有必要在胜利当晚把自己锁在家里，以避免遭遇暴力。”她说，球员们将参加周日下午的胜利游行，其中包括参观埃菲尔铁塔旁边的战神广场，以及法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙举办的招待会。图片周六晚上，照明弹照亮了香榭丽舍大街。
