articles.title: Immigrant detainees sue over 'horrific' conditions at Texas ICE facility
articles.summary: Immigrant detainees accuse the federal government of 'inhumane' abuse and treatment at Camp East Montana in El Paso, Texas.
articles.titleZh: 移民被拘留者因德克萨斯州 ICE 设施的“可怕”条件提起诉讼
articles.summaryZh: 移民被拘留者指责联邦政府在德克萨斯州埃尔帕索的东蒙大拿营地实施“不人道”的虐待和待遇。

contentParagraphs.en: A sign marks the entrance to a series of hardened tents at the Camp East Montana immigrant detention center in the desert at a U.S. Army base on the outskirts of El Paso, Texas, Feb. 13, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 2 月 13 日，得克萨斯州埃尔帕索郊区的美国陆军基地，东蒙大拿州移民拘留中心的一系列加固帐篷的入口处有一个标志。

contentParagraphs.en: Four detainees at the largest Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention center in the U.S. filed a federal lawsuit on Saturday alleging human rights abuses, "horrific" conditions and "severe medical neglect" at the facility.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国最大的移民和海关执法拘留中心的四名被拘留者于周六提起联邦诉讼，指控该设施侵犯人权、“可怕”的条件和“严重的医疗忽视”。

contentParagraphs.en: The complaint, filed in the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas, details "inhumane" treatment inside Camp East Montana on the U.S. Army's Fort Bliss military base in El Paso, Texas. The suit describes a litany of abuse allegations, including a lack of medical care and physical violence at the hands of guards, and accuses the government of human rights and constitutional violations.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该诉状向美国德克萨斯州西区地方法院提起，详细描述了位于德克萨斯州埃尔帕索的美国陆军布利斯堡军事基地东蒙大拿营内的“不人道”待遇。该诉讼描述了一系列虐待指控，包括缺乏医疗服务和警卫的身体暴力，并指责政府侵犯人权和违反宪法。

contentParagraphs.en: "Detained people are regularly subjected to severe beatings or sexual harassment by guards; squalid living conditions; spoiled and inadequate food; no meaningful programming or recreation; inadequate access to basic hygiene products such as soap, razors or nail clippers, outbreaks of disease; and limited or no access to sunlight," according to the complaint.
contentParagraphs.zh: 投诉称：“被拘留者经常遭受看守的严厉殴打或性骚扰；生活条件肮脏；食物变质且不足；没有有意义的节目或娱乐；无法获得肥皂、剃须刀或指甲刀等基本卫生用品，疾病暴发；接触阳光的机会有限或无法接触。”

contentParagraphs.en: It's the first lawsuit against the facility. Immigration advocates and former detainees have been calling for the massive facility to be shut down for months.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这是针对该设施的第一起诉讼。几个月来，移民倡导者和前被拘留者一直呼吁关闭这个大型设施。

contentParagraphs.en: The plaintiffs filed the suit on behalf of themselves, all detainees of the facility and future people held there. They're seeking class-action status for the legal challenge.
contentParagraphs.zh: 原告代表自己、该设施的所有被拘留者以及未来被关押在那里的人提起诉讼。他们正在寻求集体诉讼以应对法律挑战。

contentParagraphs.en: According to the complaint, the center's guards beat Gerald Akari Angye, one of the named plaintiffs, so severely he had to be hospitalized and placed in a wheelchair. Angye, who has been at Camp East Montana for just over a month, claims he was then locked in solitary confinement for 15 days.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据诉状，该中心的警卫殴打了原告之一杰拉尔德·阿卡里·安吉(Gerald Akari Angye)，殴打严重，导致他不得不住院并坐在轮椅上。安吉在蒙大拿州东营才呆了一个多月，他声称自己随后被单独监禁了 15 天。

contentParagraphs.en: "No human being should ever have to go through this," Angye said in a statement released by the American Civil Liberties Union, one of the organizations representing the detainees. "I have already experienced torture in my home country of Cameroon and I never thought I would experience such severely violent treatment by guards here in the United States of America."
contentParagraphs.zh: 安吉在代表被拘留者的组织之一美国公民自由联盟发表的一份声明中说：“任何人都不应该经历这一切。” “我已经在我的祖国喀麦隆经历过酷刑，我从未想过我会在美利坚合众国遭受警卫如此严重的暴力对待。”

contentParagraphs.en: Another detainee identified in the complaint only as Navdeep, a former mail handler with no criminal history, says he experienced dirty toilet water flowing into his sleeping area, difficulty accessing cups for drinking water and breathing problems because of excessive dust from the desert. Navdeep wore the same clothes, including underwear, for three weeks, according to the lawsuit.
contentParagraphs.zh: 申诉中的另一名被拘留者纳夫迪普（Navdeep）是一名没有犯罪记录的前邮件处理员，他说，他经历过肮脏的厕所水流入他的睡眠区，难以拿取饮用水的杯子，并且由于沙漠灰尘过多而出现呼吸问题。诉讼称，纳维迪普连续三周都穿着同样的衣服，包括内衣。

contentParagraphs.en: "We could die here, and it feels like no one here would care," Navdeep said in the ACLU statement.
contentParagraphs.zh: 纳夫迪普在美国公民自由联盟的声明中说：“我们可能会死在这里，但感觉这里没有人关心。”

contentParagraphs.en: People being held at Camp East Montana don't receive timely medications to manage a range of serious medical issues such as HIV, cancer and diabetes, according to the lawsuit. In February, the detention center was closed temporarily to visitors because of a measles outbreak, according to Marfa Public Radio. The complaint also describes housing units without windows, crammed spaces and a constant odor of urine and feces, a lack of clean water and reports that detainees receive only two pieces of bread, a piece of ham or bologna, a slice of cheese and a cookie for all three meals.
contentParagraphs.zh: 诉讼称，被关押在蒙大拿州东部营地的人们无法及时获得药物来治疗艾滋病毒、癌症和糖尿病等一系列严重的医疗问题。据马尔法公共广播电台报道，二月份，由于麻疹爆发，拘留中心暂时对访客关闭。投诉还描述了没有窗户的住房单元、拥挤的空间、持续的尿液和粪便气味、缺乏干净的水，并报告称被拘留者的三餐只得到两片面包、一块火腿或博洛尼亚、一片奶酪和一块饼干。

contentParagraphs.en: ICE Director Todd Lyons and Department of Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin are named as defendants in the lawsuit. In an email to NPR, a DHS spokesperson who would not give their name, rebutted the claims in the lawsuit saying they are "categorically false."
contentParagraphs.zh: ICE 主管托德·莱昂斯 (Todd Lyons) 和国土安全部部长马克韦恩·穆林 (Markwayne Mullin) 被列为诉讼被告。在给美国公共广播电台的一封电子邮件中，一位不愿透露姓名的国土安全部发言人反驳了诉讼中的说法，称这些说法“绝对是错误的”。

contentParagraphs.en: "ICE is regularly audited and inspected by external agencies to ensure that all ICE facilities comply with performance-based national detention standards. All detainees are provided with proper meals, quality water, blankets, medical treatment, and have opportunities to communicate with their family members and lawyers. ICE has higher detention standards than most U.S. prisons that hold actual U.S. citizens."
contentParagraphs.zh: “ICE 定期接受外部机构的审计和检查，以确保所有 ICE 设施符合基于绩效的国家拘留标准。所有被拘留者都获得适当的膳食、优质水、毯子、医疗，并有机会与家人和律师沟通。ICE 的拘留标准比大多数关押美国公民的美国监狱更高。”

contentParagraphs.en: Camp East Montana is a sprawling encampment of tents in the Chihuahuan Desert opened in 2025. It has the capacity to hold up to 5,000 people but usually houses about 3,000.
contentParagraphs.zh: 东蒙大拿营地是奇瓦瓦沙漠中的一个大型帐篷营地，于 2025 年开放。它最多可容纳 5,000 人，但通常可容纳 3,000 人左右。

contentParagraphs.en: At least three people have died at the center, including Cuban national Gerald Lunas Campos, according to previous NPR reporting. The El Paso County Medical Examiner's Office ruled Campos' death a homicide and no one has been charged. In February, ICE found 49 violations to detention standards at center, including inadequate medical care and failure from staff to "accurately document required checks to prevent significant self-harm and suicide." DHS has disputed those claims.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据美国国家公共广播电台 (NPR) 此前报道，该中心至少有三人死亡，其中包括古巴国民杰拉尔德·卢纳斯·坎波斯 (Gerald Lunas Campos)。埃尔帕索县法医办公室裁定坎波斯的死亡为他杀，目前尚未有人受到指控。今年 2 月，ICE 发现该中心存在 49 起违反拘留标准的行为，包括医疗护理不足以及工作人员未能“准确记录必要的检查以防止重大自残和自杀”。国土安全部对这些说法提出了异议。

contentParagraphs.en: Several members of Congress have conducted unannounced oversight visits to the detention center. Minnesota Congresswoman Kelly Morrison, a Democrat, visited in March after ICE detained thousands of people from her state and flew them to the encampment during the federal crackdown targeting Minneapolis. Morrison said she was horrified by the cruelty she witnessed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 几名国会议员对拘留中心进行了未经事先通知的监督视察。明尼苏达州众议员、民主党人凯莉·莫里森 (Kelly Morrison) 今年 3 月访问了该州，此前 ICE 在针对明尼阿波利斯的联邦镇压行动中拘留了该州数千人，并将他们空运到营地。莫里森说，她对亲眼目睹的残酷行为感到震惊。

articles.title: Bound by blood: new film highlights Jamaica’s outlawed obeah belief system
articles.summary:
Stew Peas focuses on obeah, an enduring African magic practice in Jamaica banned by colonisers in the 1700s
A new movie from award-winning Jamaican film-maker Sosiessia Nixon shines a spotlight on Jamaica’s enduring west African-based magic and spiritual healing tradition known a
articles.titleZh: 血缘相连：新电影凸显牙买加非法的奥比信仰体系
articles.summaryZh:
《Stew Peas》专注于 Obeah，这是牙买加一种经久不衰的非洲魔法实践，在 1700 年代被殖民者禁止
屡获殊荣的牙买加电影制片人 Sosiessia Nixon 的一部新电影聚焦于牙买加持久的西非魔法和精神治疗传统。

contentParagraphs.en: A new movie from award-winning Jamaican film-maker Sosiessia Nixon shines a spotlight on Jamaica’s enduring west African-based magic and spiritual healing tradition known as obeah.Nixon’s tense, feature-length suspense, Stew Peas, tells of the story of Jamaican detective Tessa, who is obsessed with an old murder case.Tessa’s life begins to fall apart when it becomes clear that her husband, Neil, has fallen under the spell of her new maid, Marcia. The story takes a dark turn with the shocking revelation that Marcia has been adding a secret ingredient to Neil’s food – her menstrual blood.“This film focuses on the persisting Jamaican obeah belief, that a woman could ‘bind’ a man in a relationship by serving him a meal of the traditional kidney beans and meat stew, which becomes a potent love potion when her menstrual blood is added,” Nixon said.Nixon hopes the movie will spark a dialogue about the tension between Christianity and obeah, which is rooted in the country’s African heritage and still practised today despite being outlawed by colonisers in the 1700s – and still illegal today.“The practice of binding a man with stew peas remains very much taboo in Jamaica, and I wanted to open a conversation. I wanted to look at this belief system in depth. Jamaicans often say that belief kills and belief cures, meaning that whatever you believe, that is what is going to happen. So, does this thing really work?” Nixon said.Coming from St Thomas, an idyllic coastal parish on the south-eastern tip of Jamaica, sometimes nicknamed the “obeah parish”, Nixon said she was inspired by actual experiences.“Growing up in St Thomas, I was very much exposed to a lot of obeah,” Nixon said.Producer and actor, Ava Eagle Brown, who created Jamaica’s Black River film festival, said the film will resonate with Caribbean people everywhere. “There is so much of us in this film, the things that make us Jamaican – especially if you’re in the diaspora … it brings you back home.”Brown, who is also in the film, added: “It’s probably going to now have some men looking at their woman with suspicion and asking: ‘What did you put it in my stew peas?’” she said. “But on a serious level, I told my son to make sure he doesn’t eat any stew peas from any woman!”Sonjah Stanley Niaah, a Jamaican cultural studies scholar and the director for UWI’s Centre for Reparation Research, said the stew peas belief is linked to the African view that natural elements, including blood from menstruation, has an inherent potency. The idea, she added, was that the red kidney beans will mask the blood so the man being charmed cannot detect it.Stanley Niaah welcomed the opportunity to explore forms of African spiritualities, which she said are often misunderstood, after being vilified and outlawed by European colonialists who had linked them to resistance and rebellions among enslaved Africans.“People in this part of the world are people of African descent and there’s a pantheon of African spirituality that we have in our blood, that we have inherited … But [today], African spirituality has no attention, no substance, it’s not being taught in schools, we are so afraid of ourselves, we are neglecting it,” she said.She added: “What we now have is this very profound, alive and longstanding tension between Christian practices and African spirituality. Enslavement was sanctioned by the church. So, some aspects of the legislative architecture in the Caribbean were certainly driven by the need to have enslaved people not assemble, or gather for any reason, whether to worship their gods or to plan rebellions. This legislative architecture is very much present even today, when you see the Obeah Act still on the books in Jamaica.”Jamaica needs to keep making films that boldly represent the region, communities and cultures, even as it grapples with tough challenges such as rebuilding after Hurricane Melissa, Stanley Niaah said.Brown, who had to cancel this year’s film festival after Hurricane Melissa demolished parts of Black River, where the event is normally held, echoed Stanley Niaah’s sentiments, describing Stew Peas as “a ray of hope”, as Jamaica’s multibillion-dollar creative industry struggles to recover.“This year I had to postpone the Black River film festival, which was a real blow because it was part of how Jamaican creatives were starting to connect with the globe, including contacts from major networks like Canal+ and Netflix,” she said.She added: “The hurricane destroyed so much! It destroyed infrastructure, equipment and for some people it destroyed hope. And that is why we need projects like this that demonstrate the resilience of Jamaicans, and send a message to the world that we are still making music and movies and adding that quintessential Jamaican green, gold and black hue to entertainment.”Jamaica’s film commissioner Jackie Jacqueline Jackson said films such as Stew Peas are “a powerful testament to the resilience, ingenuity, and determination of Jamaica’s creative industry”.“It’s important to keep going and demonstrate that Jamaica is still open for business. By signalling this, it encourages international productions to return to Jamaica which positively affects jobs and film production expenditure,” she added.
contentParagraphs.zh: 屡获殊荣的牙买加电影制片人索西西娅·尼克松 (Sosiessia Nixon) 的一部新片聚焦于牙买加经久不衰的西非魔法和精神治疗传统，即“Obeah”。尼克松的紧张悬疑长片《炖豌豆》讲述了牙买加侦探泰莎 (Tessa) 的故事，她痴迷于一起古老的谋杀案。当泰莎 (Tessa) 的生活开始分崩离析时，她的丈夫尼尔 (Neil) 发现了被她的新女仆玛西娅迷住了。故事发生了黑暗的转折，令人震惊的是，玛西娅在尼尔的食物中添加了一种秘密成分——她的经血。尼克松说：“这部电影聚焦于牙买加持续存在的奥比信仰，即女人可以通过为男人提供一顿传统的芸豆和炖肉来‘束缚’一个男人，当她的经血被添加后，这顿饭就会成为一剂强效的爱情药水。”尼克松希望这部电影能够引发一场关于基督教与基督教之间紧张关系的对话。 “用炖豌豆捆绑一个人的做法在牙买加仍然是非常禁忌的，我想展开对话。我想深入研究这个信仰体系。牙买加人经常说信仰杀人，信仰治愈，这意味着无论你相信什么，都会发生什么。所以，这件事是吗？真的有用吗？”尼克松来自牙买加东南端一个田园诗般的沿海教区圣托马斯，有时被昵称为“奥比教区”，尼克松说，她的灵感来自于实际经历。“在圣托马斯长大，我接触过很多奥比教区，”尼克松说。制片人兼演员、牙买加黑河电影节的创始人艾娃·伊格尔·布朗表示，这部电影将引起世界各地加勒比人民的共鸣。 “这部电影中有很多人，那些让我们成为牙买加人的东西——尤其是如果你身在海外……它会让你回到家乡。”也在电影中的布朗补充道：“现在可能会有一些男人怀疑地看着他们的女人并问：‘你在我的豌豆里放了什么？’”她说。 “但严肃地说，我告诉我的儿子，确保他不要吃任何女人的炖豌豆！” 牙买加文化研究学者、西印度大学赔偿研究中心主任索尼亚·斯坦利·尼亚阿 (Sonjah Stanley Niaah) 表示，炖豌豆信仰与非洲人的观点有关，即包括经血在内的自然元素具有固有的效力。她补充说，这个想法是红芸豆会掩盖血液，这样被施咒的人就无法察觉到它。斯坦利·尼亚阿（Stanley Niaah）欢迎有机会探索非洲灵性的形式，她说非洲灵性在受到欧洲殖民主义者的诽谤和取缔后经常被误解，因为欧洲殖民者将它们与被奴役的非洲人的抵抗和叛乱联系起来。她说：“我们继承了……但今天，非洲灵性没有受到关注，没有实质内容，学校没有教授它，我们太害怕自己了，我们忽视了它。”她补充说：“我们现在面临的是基督教习俗和非洲灵性之间存在着这种非常深刻、活跃和长期的紧张关系，奴役是受到教会认可的。因此，加勒比地区立法架构的某些方面肯定是因为需要让被奴役的人不聚集或聚集。即使在今天，当你看到牙买加的《奥比法案》仍然存在时，这种立法架构仍然存在。“牙买加需要继续制作大胆代表该地区、社区和文化的电影，即使它正在应对飓风梅丽莎后的重建等严峻挑战。”斯坦利·尼亚赫说。在飓风梅丽莎摧毁了黑河的部分地区后，布朗不得不取消今年的电影节。斯坦利·尼亚赫 (Stanley Niaah) 的观点呼应了这项活动的正常举行，她将《炖豌豆》描述为“一线希望”，因为牙买加价值数十亿美元的创意产业正在努力复苏。“今年我不得不推迟黑河电影节，这是一个真正的打击，因为这是牙买加创意人员开始与全球联系的一部分，包括与 Canal+ 和 Netflix 等主要网络的联系，”她说。它摧毁了基础设施、设备，对一些人来说，它摧毁了希望，这就是为什么我们需要这样的项目来展示牙买加人的韧性，并向世界传达一个信息：我们仍在制作音乐和电影，并为娱乐增添典型的牙买加绿色、金色和黑色色调。”牙买加电影专员杰基·杰奎琳·杰克逊表示，《炖豌豆》等电影“有力地证明了牙买加人的韧性、创造力和决心。”创意产业”。“重要的是继续前进并表明牙买加仍然对商业开放，通过发出这一信号，它鼓励国际制作返回牙买加，这对就业和电影制作支出产生积极影响，”她补充道。
